title: 138 Combat Engagements March 21; ISW Confirms Russia's Spring Offensive Is Underway
detail: Ukraine's General Staff recorded 138 combat engagements on March 21, 2026 (Day 1,487). Russia conducted 48 airstrikes, deployed 3,512 kamikaze drones, and fired 2,375 artillery shells. Russian losses for the day: 940 personnel killed or wounded. Active combat zones: Pokrovsk direction (28 attacks), Kostiantynivka direction (17 assaults), Kupyansk direction (5 attacks toward Petropavlivka and Kurilivka), Lyman direction (3 repelled assaults near Stavka and Dibrova), South-Slobozhansky (5 assaults near Starytsia and Okhrimivka). ISW formally assessed that Russia had likely initiated its anticipated Spring-Summer 2026 offensive against Ukraine's 'Fortress Belt' — the 50-km fortified chain from Sloviansk through Kramatorsk to Druzhkivka and Kostiantynivka. ISW assessed Russia is unlikely to seize the entire Fortress Belt in 2026 and any gains will come at significant cost.

media.caption: ISW confirms Russia's spring offensive against Ukraine's Fortress Belt is underway

title: Ukraine-US Bilateral Meeting in Miami; Ukraine Proposes Drone Technology Cooperation
detail: Ukrainian and US officials held bilateral meetings in Miami, Florida on March 21-22, with both sides describing discussions as constructive. Ukraine presented the Trump administration with a proposal for expanded drone cooperation, centered on sharing Ukraine's battle-tested counter-drone technology — specifically systems designed to intercept Iranian-made Shahed drones. The proposal reflects Ukraine's interest in monetizing its drone warfare experience and deepening US-Ukraine technological cooperation as an alternative to direct weapons transfers. Separately, ISW assessed that February 2026 was the first month since 2024 in which Ukraine regained more territory than it lost — representing a significant turning point amid the intensifying spring offensive.

media.caption: Ukrainian delegation arrives in Miami for bilateral talks with US officials
